sections.about: This metric measures export revenue as a percentage of total turnover and is disclosed in BRSR Section A — Operations, “Markets served by the entity.” It is disclosed under Section A, Operations, and is not a Principle 1–9 Essential or Leadership indicator. A higher value indicates better performance for this benchmarking lens because it signals broader geographic market access and lower dependence on only domestic offtake.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Jindal Stainless led all three years, rising from 15.49% in FY2022-23 to 17.07% in FY2023-24, then moderating to 11.69% in FY2024-25; its FY2024-25 BRSR also states 49 international countries served and export contribution of ~11.69%, consistent with its stainless-steel product positioning and higher global tradability. JSW increased from 9% to 12% before falling to 5.5%, indicating a swing back toward domestic realisations or weaker export competitiveness in FY2024-25. Tata declined from 7% to 6% and stayed at 6%; Tata’s FY2024-25 BRSR explains that it serves international clients through subsidiaries but separately discloses direct India exports, with standalone export revenue of ₹7,700.45 crore against total revenue of ₹132,516.66 crore, or 6%. SAIL’s export share steadily reduced from 2.56% to 1.61% and 0.48%, consistent with its public-sector integrated carbon-steel profile and stronger domestic-market orientation.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranked 2nd at 6%, behind Jindal Stainless at 11.69%. Tata’s gap from the best performer is 5.69 percentage points, or about 48.7% below Jindal on a relative basis. Tata’s rank improved from 3rd in FY2022-23 and FY2023-24 to 2nd in FY2024-25 because JSW’s export contribution fell sharply to 5.5%, while Tata remained stable at 6%. Strategically, Tata’s low-to-moderate export share should not be read as weak market capability; the BRSR notes that Tata serves international customers through subsidiaries, so direct standalone export percentage captures only part of Tata Steel’s international market exposure.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Tata Steel’s FY2024-25 BRSR distinguishes consolidated sales outside India from standalone exports from India; the benchmark uses the standalone export percentage for comparability. Tata FY2022-23 is consolidated, which limits strict comparability with standalone peers.
